_comment: Per-partner fixture content. `sources` is the raw text the live LLM would see; `_extraction` is the canned PartnerSignalsOutput used in stub mode.

sources.text: On the Distribution podcast Priya Anand discussed where she invests at seed. Quote: 'Compliance reporting is the wedge nobody wants to build but everyone regulated has to buy eventually.' She also said Northbeam looks for fintech infrastructure that survives regulatory scrutiny.
In a Northbeam blog post Priya wrote: 'We invest in the picks and shovels of regulated finance - the APIs that make compliance a feature, not a project.'

signals.quoted_text: Compliance reporting is the wedge nobody wants to build but everyone regulated has to buy eventually.
We invest in the picks and shovels of regulated finance - the APIs that make compliance a feature, not a project.

reachability_signals.evidence: Active podcast appearance in 2026-02; recent fund blog post in 2026-04.

_extraction.cold_reachability_reasoning: Recent public output and explicit willingness to discuss thesis in podcast format.

sources.text: Marcus Lindqvist on the DevFin podcast: 'Reconciliation infrastructure is the most boring and most valuable thing in fintech right now - APIs that do not lose pennies.'

signals.quoted_text: Reconciliation infrastructure is the most boring and most valuable thing in fintech right now - APIs that do not lose pennies.

_extraction.cold_reachability_reasoning: Some public output; less prolific than Anand.

sources.text: Dana Cole wrote in a Tidewater post: 'B2B founders at seed get more signal from five design partners doing real work than from a thousand sign-ups.'
On the Ops Podcast Dana said: 'We back founder-led sales because that is the only honest signal at seed.'

signals.quoted_text: B2B founders at seed get more signal from five design partners doing real work than from a thousand sign-ups.
We back founder-led sales because that is the only honest signal at seed.

reachability_signals.evidence: Recent blog and podcast appearances in last 6 months.

_extraction.cold_reachability_reasoning: Consistent recent public output across multiple formats.

sources.text: Renee Park published: 'If a seed founder is not doing sales themselves, I worry they do not understand the buyer.'

signals.quoted_text: If a seed founder is not doing sales themselves, I worry they do not understand the buyer.

sources.text: Sofia Reyes on DeepTechCast: 'Hard tech timing is everything - you are either three years too early or three years too late, and getting the window right matters more than the team.'
Quanta Labs post by Sofia: 'The interesting deep-tech work is happening at the photonics-software interface - infrastructure, not application.'

signals.quoted_text: Hard tech timing is everything - you are either three years too early or three years too late, and getting the window right matters more than the team.
The interesting deep-tech work is happening at the photonics-software interface - infrastructure, not application.

reachability_signals.evidence: Two recent public posts in 6 months.

_extraction.cold_reachability_reasoning: Moderate public footprint; pre-seed deep tech is niche but reachable.

sources.text: Kwame Boateng on the Climate Podcast: 'Climate is a policy-shaped market - when subsidies and mandates land, they create forced-buy windows worth underwriting.'
Foundry North post by Kwame: 'Grid software needs to be built with regulation as a feature, not a constraint.'

signals.quoted_text: Climate is a policy-shaped market - when subsidies and mandates land, they create forced-buy windows worth underwriting.
Grid software needs to be built with regulation as a feature, not a constraint.

reachability_signals.evidence: Two recent public posts in last 4 months.

_extraction.cold_reachability_reasoning: Active public footprint in climate-policy discourse.

sources.text: Ingrid Solberg published: 'The decarbonization customers who matter are the ones forced to buy by upcoming compliance deadlines.'

signals.quoted_text: The decarbonization customers who matter are the ones forced to buy by upcoming compliance deadlines.
